w:r.w:t: Prepared by: Ayush Saha, Founder & CEO | ayush@flowfiy.com | flowfiy.com
STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL -- For Authorized Investors Only

The $4.8B outbound sales automation market is dominated by tools that charge a premium to absorb API costs. Flowfiy inverts this model: Flowfiy manages Claude centrally, giving Flowfiy ~70-80% gross margins from day one -- while delivering Clay-level AI intelligence at Apollo-level pricing. This structural cost advantage creates a near-unassailable moat.
Near-zero COGS: managed AI model eliminates Claude API cost exposure. ~$0.002 infra cost per full lead generation pipeline.
Defensible moat: 5 specialized AI agents working in concert (ICP -> Lead Discovery -> Company Analysis -> Qualification -> Personalization) cannot be replicated by a single-prompt competitor.
Global market, local advantage: Competing globally but launching from India enables 5-10x cost efficiency vs. US-based competitors building similar products.
Proven demand signals: Clay's $1.5B valuation and 50,000+ users proves the market. Flowfiy enters at 3-10x lower price point with better AI and higher margins.
Bootstrappable to $25K MRR: 15 Starter subscribers break even on infra. $300K pre-seed is acceleration capital, not survival capital.
Product: 5-agent Claude AI pipeline for B2B outbound sales automation
Business Model: Multi-tenant SaaS, Managed Claude AI, Seat + Generation pricing with token budgets
Stage: Pre-seed, product built, zero paid users
Founded: 2025 by Ayush Saha, Pune, India
Target Market: Global -- agencies, SaaS startups, outbound sales teams
V1 Channel: Gmail outreach (LinkedIn + WhatsApp in V2 roadmap)
Tech Stack: Next.js 14, Supabase (PostgreSQL + RLS), BullMQ, Stripe, Railway/Vercel
Agent 1 -- ICP Analyzer: Identifies and refines Ideal Customer Profile from business inputs
Agent 2 -- Lead Discovery: Queries Apollo.io API for matching leads at scale
Agent 3 -- Company Analyzer: Apify web scraping for deep company research per lead
Agent 4 -- Qualification Agent: Scores and ranks leads against ICP criteria (Claude Haiku)
Agent 5 -- Personalization Agent: Generates hyper-personalized email copy (Claude Sonnet)
450 paid users x $75 ARPU x 12 months
Expand to 6,500 users, agency + white-label tier
Clay: $1.5B valuation, $50M ARR. Proves the AI enrichment market at $149-720/mo price point.
Apollo.io: $1.6B valuation, $100M ARR. Proves the outbound data market at $49-119/mo.
Instantly: $100M estimated valuation. Proves the cold email automation market at $37-97/mo.
Flowfiy addressable gap: AI-native + managed Claude + affordable pricing for the 95% who won’t pay $149+/mo for Clay.
Month 12: $33,244 MRR (449 paid users) -- $398K ARR run-rate
Monthly churn: 3% (industry average for SMB SaaS: 3-5%)
Free-to-paid conversion: 40% within 90 days of sign-up
Growth rate: 30-40% MoM Months 1-6; 18-25% MoM Months 7-12
$1M ARR requires ~1,111 paid subscribers at $75 ARPU -- achievable by Month 18-20
At $1M ARR with 95% gross margin: $950K gross profit annually
Comparable: Instantly reached ~$10M ARR bootstrapped; Flowfiy has better margins

Traditional SaaS tools absorb LLM API costs and pass them on as subscription fees, limiting gross margins to 50-70%. Flowfiy's managed AI model means every dollar of Claude API cost is borne by the user -- delivering ~70-80% gross margin from day one. This is not a feature, it is a structural economic moat.
Early entry with 95% GM + Managed AI moat
Comparable pre-seed SaaS B2B valuations in India: $1.5M-$5M pre-money for product-live stage
managed AI model creates near-100% gross margin -- rare at any stage, exceptional at pre-seed
Clay's $1.5B at $50M ARR (30x) = early entry point in same category is highly justified
$3M pre-money = 0.06x of Clay's current valuation, with same market, better economics
Modest pre-money ($3M vs $5M+ for comparable US startups) reflects India-based discount

Seeking $300K at $3M pre-money valuation (10% equity). This is not survival capital -- Flowfiy breaks even on infrastructure at 15 Starter subscribers. This is acceleration capital to hire one engineer, fund marketing, and reach $400K ARR in 12 months.
YouTube channel, paid ads, SEO sprints
Terms of service, privacy policy, trademark
Instrument: SAFE (Simple Agreement for Future Equity) -- standard YC post-money SAFE
Pro-rata rights: Yes, up to initial investment amount
Information rights: Quarterly financials, monthly MRR updates
Target close date: Within 45 days of term sheet
Board seat: Observer rights only at pre-seed stage
Builder: Designed and built entire Flowfiy platform solo (Next.js, Supabase, BullMQ, Stripe, multi-tenant architecture)
Technical depth: Full-stack engineering -- React, TypeScript, PostgreSQL, Node.js, AI/LLM orchestration
Domain expertise: Deep understanding of outbound sales automation, GTM tooling, and B2B SaaS economics
Execution proof: Shipped production-grade multi-tenant SaaS with RLS, queue workers, billing, and 5-agent AI pipeline independently

Building a product of this technical complexity solo -- multi-tenant architecture, encrypted secret storage, AI orchestration with queue workers, Stripe billing -- is strong evidence of both technical ability and execution velocity. The founder is the technical moat.
Managed AI means cost is user-borne; add OpenAI/Gemini fallback in V2
Compete on price ($49 vs $149+), ease of use, and India/APAC market positioning
Usage-triggered upgrade prompts, email nurture, time-limited free boost offers
Modular integration layer -- swap providers without rebuilding core pipeline
Use $120K engineer budget to distribute critical engineering knowledge
Public beta launch (Product Hunt + LinkedIn + HN)
Engineer contract signed, V2 LinkedIn integration scoped
Seed round preparation: target $1.5M at $10M valuation
Clay is a $1.5B company. Apollo is a $1.6B company.
Both charge users for the AI compute they consume.
Flowfiy doesn't. That's the entire thesis.
